title: Easy Healthy Homemade Watermelon Sorbet (Only 3 Ingredients)

chunks.title: Introduction to Watermelon Sorbet
chunks.summary: The video opens with a brief introduction to making a watermelon sorbet using just three ingredients: watermelon, honey, and lime. The presenter promises to share insights on the health benefits of these ingredients, particularly regarding blood sugar and metabolic health.
chunks.transcript: all right guys in this video I'm going to show you how to make watermelon sorbet with just three ingredients it's got watermelon it's got honey it's got lime and all of those things are healthy if you worry about these things I'm going to tell you all about the science in this video of why you shouldn't fear watermelon when it comes to your blood sugar I'm going to tell you a little bit about the science regarding honey and improvements and metabolic Health which are astounding and I'm going to show you how to make this super simple dessert which your girlfriend wife grandma grandpa your kids they'll all love it it's really good and it's beautiful let's get into it I'm feeling like I'm uh starting a cooking show so you guys let me know what you think watermelon

chunks.title: Health Benefits of Watermelon
chunks.summary: The presenter discusses the misconceptions about watermelon and its effects on blood sugar levels, providing research findings that demonstrate how watermelon can actually improve insulin sensitivity. He emphasizes that watermelon is a healthy addition to the diet and gives advice on carbohydrate intake based on activity levels.
chunks.transcript: sorbet let me show you how to do it all right three ingredients in the watermelon sorbet watermelon honey preferably raw organic glyphosate free and lime I'm going to show you guys how to make this but before I do I know a lot of you are going to look at this video and go that is a lot of sugar Paul aren't you worried about that the answer is no for a couple of reasons look scale the amount of carbohydrates in your diet based on how active you are and realize that there are very good studies with watermelon and watermelon juice showing that watermelon juice and watermelons actually improve insulin sensitivity researchers have given patients watermelon juice in the setting of a glucose tolerance test they're giving people pure glucose and they give them watermelon juice and their insulin sensitivity their tolerance of that glucose actually improves when they give them more sugar with the watermelon juice so do I think that watermelon is going to cause diabetes no do I think that watermelon or watermelon juice is going to worsen diabetes no do I think that you should scale the amount of carbohydrates in your diet based on your activity sure does everybody need to eat 300 grams of carbohydrates a day no but I do believe that watermelon is a healthy addition to the human diet and I think that the medical studies show that

chunks.title: Understanding Honey's Role in Diet
chunks.summary: Honey is introduced as a common concern for diabetics; however, the presenter explains studies showing that honey can improve glucose tolerance. He stresses that metabolic dysfunction is a more significant issue than elevated blood sugar and suggests that moderate amounts of honey can be beneficial, especially for metabolically healthy individuals.
chunks.transcript: limes pretty benign for most people honey is another uh lightning rod for people and let me tell you about honey real quickly honey is something that people think is going to worsen their diabetes though it may raise your blood sugar slightly even if you're diabetic there are actual randomized control trials with humans and honey up to 125 grams plus of honey per day in diabetics showing that over an 8we period administering honey actually improves glucose tolerance blood sugar goes up a little bit but it improves glucose tolerance remember this guys diabetes insulin resistance are not diseases of elevated blood sugar that is a symptom that is not the cause C they are diseases of insulin resistance metabolic dysfunction and honey watermelon these do not cause metabolic dysfunction if you have metabolic dysfunction scale these based on your activity level But realize that these can still be a healthy part of your diet and I believe that for those of us that are metabolically healthy adding honey to your diet in moderate amounts can be a very very powerful thing post exercise I

chunks.title: Seedless vs Seeded Watermelon
chunks.summary: The video transitions to a discussion about the difference between seedless and seeded watermelons. The presenter expresses a preference for seeded watermelon, citing taste differences and the naturalness of viable seeds, while mentioning that seedless watermelons are not genetically modified but have undergone manipulation.
chunks.transcript: thing to make this sorbet guys you cut open the watermelon Tada then I'm going to try and scoop this out with a spoon to see if I can end up with a very pretty Bowl to serve this sorban afterwards let's see how I do here I'm going to take all these pieces and freeze them for 2 to three hours one more thing that I think about with watermelon is seedless versus seed containing watermelon I think that watermelon with seeds tastes way better and while seedless watermelon is not technically GMO it has been kind of uh manipulated so uh it gets technical but watermelon with seeds that you can actually plant in the ground are deployed they have two sets of chromosomes these watermelon seeds are actually viable if I plant these watermelon seeds it will turn into a watermelon vine maybe it'll be Jack in the beanock and I'll be able to climb up to a huge new realm but it'll probably just be a watermelon vine and I'll get a watermelon seedless watermelons have some funny stuff going on with their chromosomes they generally are made when you cross a tetrol watermelon that is a watermelon with four sets of chromosomes relative to a diploid which has two if you cross a tetrol watermelon with a diploid watermelon you get a triploid or tripod watermelon so a seedless watermelon is essentially a triod watermelon now there aren't any studies showing that seedless watermelon are harmful for humans but all that's a little bit kind of like weird with the chromosomes and I think that the uh the

chunks.title: Making Watermelon Sorbet
chunks.summary: The presenter demonstrates the process of making the watermelon sorbet, starting by freezing watermelon pieces and blending them with honey and lime juice. He shares tips on using the right equipment and the importance of consistency in the sorbet's texture.
chunks.transcript: let's pull it out of the freezer we got some frozen watermelons slices here these are going to go into the blender I would not recommend trying this without a f blender you will burn the motor out maybe Vitamix works good just so you guys know I'm going to use the plastic container on this Vitamix so you can see it because it looks better for the video but I would recommend using the stainless steel attachment of the Vitamix because you don't want extra plastic in your food this is just for the video um but Vitamix also sells a stainless steel attachment and this is what I use for everything that I'm making for myself so this is just for you guys try and stuff as many pieces of watermelon in there as I can and I'm going to throw in some lineage raw organic glyphosate free honey not going to be conservative with that because we love it and throw in some lime juice all right let's blend it up feel like a little cooking show over here okay next step I'm going to take this put it in a glass container put it back in the freezer for a little bit so it gets to be the right consistency I'm going step ahead of myself look at this

chunks.title: Final Thoughts on Sorbet and Diet
chunks.summary: After tasting the finished watermelon sorbet, the presenter reiterates its benefits for those who are lactose intolerant and emphasizes that the three ingredients are healthy choices. He also briefly mentions the concept of an animal-based diet, describing it in relation to making other desserts.
chunks.transcript: guys we got one right here here we go looks good so there's a technique to making this look pretty and I'm probably going to butcher it but I'm going to do my best to give you guys some uh food ASMR here let's see what we can do all right there it is watermelon sorbet three ingredients let's give it a try super good legit it's definitely refreshing summertime If you're lactose intolerant and you want want to eat ice cream but you can't eat ice cream this could be a great option and remember three ingredients watermelon honey and lime I talked about it earlier in the video but you don't need to fear any of those things watermelon healthy for humans improves glucose tolerance honey tons of research about that especially when it's raw organic glyphosate free like the lineage honey it's tested and uh yeah I even made it in the watermelon bowl for you guys to make it look super pretty if you want to know how to make animal-based ice cream from raw cream and egg yolks and things like mango or dragon fruit I'll put a link to that video here I think if you can make animal- based watermelon sorbet and animal-based ice cream you're basically like a dessert ninja and you'll be able to impress anyone if you make dinner for them and just in case you guys are wondering when I say animal-based sorbet it's part of an animal-based diet which is something that I've thought of as organs meat fruit honey and raw dairy the same with the ice cream it doesn't mean this is made from animals although that would be pretty incredible if I can make something like this from animals it just means that it's within the bounds of an animal- based diet which is something I'm a huge fan of see you guys in the next video
